message:
feat(fcp-ratelimit): add smooth token bucket refill and safety guards

Improve TokenBucket rate limiter with smooth refill behavior and
protection against edge cases that could cause division by zero.

## Smooth Token Bucket Refill

Traditional token buckets exhibit "burst-then-wait" behavior: all N
tokens are added at once every T seconds, leading to traffic spikes.

New behavior normalizes the rate for smoother throughput:
- Instead of: N tokens every T seconds
- Now: 1 token every T/N seconds

This provides more consistent rate limiting without changing the
effective throughput ceiling.

```rust
// Before: 100 req/min = 100 tokens every 60s (burst then wait)
// After:  100 req/min = 1 token every 600ms (smooth flow)
```

The optimization is applied in from_config() when:
- requests_per_window > 0
- Calculated interval (window_nanos / requests) > 0
- Result fits in u64 nanoseconds

Falls back to window-based refill for extreme rates (>1 req/ns).

## Zero-Window Safety Guards

All constructors now guard against zero-duration windows:
- new(): Clamp to 1ns minimum
- from_config(): Clamp after rate normalization
- with_burst(): Clamp to 1ns minimum

This prevents division-by-zero panics from malformed configs.

## New Tests

- test_token_bucket_zero_window_safety: Verifies safe handling
  of Duration::ZERO windows

## Test Vectors

Add CBOR golden vectors for rate limiting types:
- backpressure_signal.cbor: Backpressure response encoding
- quota_state.cbor: Quota tracking state
- throttle_violation.cbor: Violation event encoding

Vectors added in both:
- crates/fcp-ratelimit/tests/vectors/rate_limiting/
- tests/vectors/rate_limiting/ (workspace-level)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
